widgets_values:
This is an example workflow, finetuned for LTX2.3 video model, using FunPack nodes to enhance and correct the results, tweaking them according to user's preferences.

Video Refiner V2 node learns from your ratings and feedback to provide the best result much more consistently than a base model does.

This workflow DOES NOT make model learn concepts it were never aware of. It only finetunes conditioning to match what YOU like as user, however it can't introduce knowledge that was never a part of a model.

This example workflow is actively using subgraphs to compact the space it takes, providing much more neat and clear workspace.

If you have any feedback or you found a bug or you have problems with using my nodes, feel free to file an issue on Github:

https://github.com/digital-garbage/ComfyUI-FunPack

Please make sure you are reporting issues while using "main" branch of the repo. Issues with "dev" branch will not be reviewed unless they are critical bugs, breaking workflow fully.

Refiner V2 Quick Guide

FunPack Video Refiner V2 is a prompt memory node. It takes your positive prompt, encodes it through the connected CLIP, remembers how you rated the previous output, and slowly learns which actions, camera moves, details, quality cues, and style cues work for your workflows.

It does not look at the finished video pixels. It does not magically know what is inside your input image. It does not replace clear prompting. In image-to-video, it now treats character appearance, clothing, subject identity, and background as image-owned by default, so it should not inject old remembered outfits or characters into a new image unless you explicitly prompt them.

How To Use It

    Add FunPack Video Refiner V2 before your sampler where positive conditioning is encoded.
    Connect your CLIP.
    Type a refinement_key, or use FunPack Refinement Key Loader and connect its output into refinement_key_input.
    First run only seeds memory. Generate once, look at the result, then rate that result on the next run with the same key.
    Use Perfect when the output is good and should be remembered.
    Use Missing action when the requested motion did not happen.
    Use Missing details when small non-identity details are weak, like smoke, reflections, particles, hand contact, object interaction, or secondary motion.
    Use Missing quality when the idea is right but the result is messy or ugly.
    Use Wrong action, Wrong details, or Wrong details + action when the video looks usable but did the wrong thing.
    Use Wrong appearance when Refiner/Lucky brought in unwanted clothing, face, body, character, subject, or background memory.
    Use -Just forget it- for broken runs, bad seeds, workflow mistakes, model failures, or anything you do not want learned.

Lucky Mode

I'm Feeling Lucky composes a prompt from learned memory. It is useful when you want the node to suggest motion, camera, detail, quality, or style ideas from previous liked outputs.

Lucky should not auto-add remembered outfits, character traits, subjects, or backgrounds anymore. If you want an appearance item, write it directly in the prompt.
Examples

If your prompt says person smoking and exhaling smoke but the video only shows a puff with no exhale, rate Missing details or Missing action, depending on what matters most. This teaches the key that smoke/exhale matters in that prompt context, without globally banning smoking.

If you liked white tights in older prompts but your new input image is only a portrait face, and Lucky tries to bring tights into the scene, rate Wrong appearance. That tells Refiner to suppress that appearance memory from auto-injection while still allowing white tights when you explicitly type it.

If the camera should push in, orbit, pan, or change framing and it stays static, rate Missing action or Wrong action. Camera movement belongs to the action/camera side of memory, so it remains safe for repair and Lucky.
Key Loader

Use FunPack Refinement Key Loader when you want a reusable key dropdown or want to move keys between workflows/machines. Its Import and Export buttons save/load the key as JSON. Connect the loader to both Refiner V2 and FunPack Apply LoRA Weights so LoRA suggestions and prompt memory use the same key.

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Positive Prompt)
widgets_values: High quality realistic video. A clean tabletop scene with a small glass sculpture, soft studio lighting, gentle camera movement, subtle reflections, natural shadows, and smooth motion. The camera slowly moves closer, then holds steady for a clear final view.

nodes.title: CLIP Text Encode (Negative Prompt)
widgets_values: low quality, blurry, distorted shapes, broken reflections, noisy shadows, overexposed highlights, undersaturated colors, bad anatomy, people, text, watermark, logo, subtitles, harsh flicker, still frame, frozen motion, frame blending artifacts, compression artifacts

nodes.title: Negative Prompt
widgets_values: music, underwater, diving, slow motion, still image, bad quality, subtitles, text, watermark, overlay, pc game, yelling, console game, childish, ugly, text, blur, logo, wordmark, static, low quality, noise, white noise, bleep, censoring, censor, bleeping, beep, beeping, newscast, interview, podcast, chinese, mutant, horror, 70's, film grain, comedy, stand-up
